identity.research_question: An integrated, reproducible computational pipeline to model overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS) in ovarian cancer by combining multi-modal patient-level data. identity.project_summary: An integrated, reproducible computational pipeline to model overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS) in ovarian cancer by combining multi-modal patient-level data. Integrates clinical labels (stage, drug combinations), genomic profiles (WES, HRD/HRP ratios, CNA status for CCNE1, MYC, MECOM from iMTB report), and multiplexed spatial proteomics features (derived from t-CycIF via SPACEstat). Employs survival models (Kaplan-Meier, Cox proportional hazards, Lasso regression), feature engineering (Variance Inflation Factor [VIF] redundancy checks, correlation heatmaps), and MOFA2 (Multi-Omics Factor Analysis) for unsupervised multi-modal integration. Spatial inputs include RCN proportions (RCN3, RCN9, RCN11), global/local immune-TSI distances, immune infiltration index (tumor vs stroma densities), nearest neighbor distance (NND), cluster density, and Gini density. Results are integrated directly with Project 12: SPACE.
identity.responsible: Venla Kaislo (Ziqi Kang, Matilda Salko, Angela Szabo, Andreas Hainari)
